title: http-post falls back to JSON-convenience + manual auth-header extraction pending full header-passing FFI
goal: lib/agent/http.esk's (http-post url headers body . timeout) currently only forwards the Authorization / x-api-key header through to the underlying http-post-json-raw FFI call (via manual assoc extraction); any other custom header in the caller's `headers` list is silently dropped because the full header-passing FFI (http-post-raw with an arbitrary header list) is not wired yet. This is a real, working fallback for the common case (bearer/API-key auth) but not a complete implementation of the documented 'headers: list of (name . value) pairs' contract.
next_actions: Wire http-post-raw (or extend http-post-json-raw) to accept and forward the full headers list, not just Authorization/x-api-key.
Update http-post in lib/agent/http.esk to pass the complete headers list through once the FFI supports it, removing the manual auth-header extraction special-case.

Add tests/agent/http_post_headers_test.esk asserting a custom header (e.g. 'X-Request-Id') set via http-post reaches the server (use the existing local HTTP test fixture server if present under tests/agent/).
acceptance: http-post forwards every (name . value) pair in its headers argument to the outgoing request, not just Authorization/x-api-key.
Existing Authorization/x-api-key-based callers keep working unchanged.
ux_payoff: Removes a documented-but-not-fully-implemented gap in the agent HTTP FFI's header contract; low urgency since the two most common auth header shapes already work.
